title: Team generation: ask-before-write on ambiguous signals, never clobber the authored seat
regression_target: The v4 generated-team flow: analysis -> capped questions -> fresh named seats with the mechanical floor (role comments, review render, provenance stamps), never-clobber, no legacy shadowing
setup: Fixture repo (schema v3) with a deliberately AMBIGUOUS PRD: it describes both card-payment processing AND patient health-record storage (two conflicting risk classes), and omits any compliance regime or quality bar. One AUTHORED QA seat exists (personas/seats/rosa.md, role qa, NO provenance stamp). No personas/amigos/ dir. Skill installed.
prompt: Run the team-generation flow for this project per reference-persona-generate.md#team: analyse, ask what you must, generate the team into personas/seats/, stamp and validate, then report. When asked the domain risk class, answer: money-movement. When asked about extra seats, accept only Security. Do not use --force; do not declare yourself headless.

expected_behaviours.description: The flow asks the domain-risk-class question BEFORE writing any card (the fixture's conflicting payments-vs-health signals make it unanswerable from analysis alone), and asks at most 4 questions total
The authored seat rosa.md is byte-identical after the run AND after a re-run; no card for role qa is generated over it (an enrichment diff may be OFFERED, never applied)
Every generated card carries a declared role comment, the review-render sections, and the provenance stamp; `validate.py seats` exits 0 on the result

The generated cards are grounded in the CHOSEN risk class: the engineering/security cards contain >=2 domain tokens from {idempotency, reconciliation, chargeback, double-charge, PCI, ledger} and zero demographic-denylist tokens
The security seat exists (accepted extra), the cast totals <=5, and the batch-accept close clears the TEAM cards' provisional stamps (persona_gen accept)

forbidden_behaviours: Writing any seat card before the risk-class question is answered
Overwriting or regenerating the authored rosa.md
More than 5 seat cards, or any card without a role comment or provenance stamp
Declaring the run headless to skip the questions
Silently picking a risk class instead of asking (the ambiguity is seeded on purpose)
grading_notes: EB1 targets the ORDER: find the first card-write in the transcript and confirm the risk-class ask precedes it; the expected question axis is domain risk class (payments vs health conflict). Scripted answers are in the prompt (money-movement; Security only). EB2 is mechanical: sha256 rosa.md before/after run and re-run. EB3 is mechanical: run validate.py seats. EB4 keyword floor is a FLOOR, not proof of coherence - also judge whether Pushes-Back-When/Shadow would actually arbitrate a payments decision (that residue is judgement, record evidence). The fixture omits compliance deliberately: asking it AFTER money-movement is chosen is correct behaviour, still within the 4-question cap.

files.sdlc-studio/prd.md:
# PRD: CarePay

> **Status:** Approved

## Overview

CarePay lets clinics take card payments at reception and stores each patient's payment history alongside their visit records.

## Features

### F1: Card payment capture

Take card payments (Visa/Mastercard) at the desk; refunds within 30 days.

### F2: Patient payment history

Store per-patient payment history with visit context for reception staff.

### F3: End-of-day settlement view

Reception leads reconcile the day's takings against the till.
files.sdlc-studio/personas/seats/rosa.md:
<!-- role: qa -->
# Rosa Delgado - QA seat

## Who They Are

Our QA lead. Hand-authored; do not regenerate.

## Craft Goals

1. Every release provably safe for reception staff.

## Lens

What breaks at the desk on a busy Monday?

## Pushes Back When

A fix ships without a till-reconciliation test.

## Shadow

Over-indexes on manual test passes.
